condition.name: Infantile Colic
overview: Infantile colic is recurrent, prolonged and inconsolable crying in an otherwise healthy, well-fed infant.
It is self-limiting, peaks at 6-8 weeks and usually resolves by 3-4 months.
Although benign, it causes significant parental anxiety and frustration.

wessels_rule_of_threes: Crying more than 3 hours per day
definition.context: Occurs in an otherwise healthy, thriving infant

proposed_etiology.notes: May be implicated in some cases

clinical_features: Crying episodes usually start in late afternoon or evening
Crying is loud, high-pitched and inconsolable
Infant may clench fists, draw legs up and appear gassy
Typically no fever, vomiting, rash or failure to thrive
history_template: Duration, timing and frequency of crying
Pattern of crying during day or evening
Any vomiting, fever, rash or poor feeding
Parental handling, emotional state and family support
Any recent formula change or overfeeding
examination_template: General appearance: alert, active and thriving
No evidence of infection or surgical cause

primary_management: Explain that colic is common, harmless and self-limiting
Validate parental emotions and provide support
soothing_techniques: White noise or gentle rhythmic sounds
feeding_advice: Avoid frequent formula changes without clear indication

medical_treatment_if_persistent_or_sever.notes: May reduce crying duration in breastfed infants
Only if cow's milk allergy suspected after ruling out red flags

parent_counseling: Colic peaks at 6-8 weeks and usually resolves by 3-4 months. — कोलिक 6-8 हफ़्ते में सबसे ज़्यादा होता है और 3-4 महीने तक अपने आप ठीक हो जाता है।
The crying is not due to poor parenting, pain or hunger in most cases. — ज़्यादातर मामलों में रोना खराब परवरिश, दर्द या भूख की वजह से नहीं होता।
There are no long-term consequences for baby's health. — बच्चे की सेहत पर इसका कोई लंबे समय का असर नहीं होता।
Parents should practice self-care, share caregiving and seek support. — माता-पिता अपना ख़्याल रखें, बारी-बारी से बच्चे को संभालें और मदद लें।
Never shake the baby. — बच्चे को कभी झटके से न हिलाएं।
warning_signs: Poor weight gain or failure to thrive
expected_course: Infantile colic is self-limiting. Crying episodes typically peak at 6-8 weeks of age and resolve spontaneously by 3-4 months in most infants. No long-term developmental or health consequences.
key_clinical_points: Colic is a diagnosis of exclusion; always rule out red flags first.
Reassurance and parental support are the mainstay of management.
No strong evidence supports any medication; simethicone and probiotics have limited benefit.
Never shake the baby -- educate parents about shaken baby syndrome risk during frustration.

severity_assessment.mild: Brief crying spells, infant consolable with soothing, feeding and weight gain normal.
severity_assessment.moderate: Prolonged crying >3 hours/day, difficult to console, but infant otherwise thriving and no red flags.
severity_assessment.severe: Excessive inconsolable crying with parental exhaustion, or any red flag symptom present requiring further evaluation.

summary_table.step: If infant is thriving and afebrile

summary_table.key_action: Limited role; only after ruling out other causes

flow_logic: Check growth and absence of red flags
If thriving and no red flags, diagnose infantile colic
Reassure parents and offer supportive care
Teach soothing techniques and review feeding
If persistent or severe, consider probiotics or formula trial
Follow-up for parental support and infant development
quick_checklist_for_opd: History of crying episodes consistent with colic?
Infant otherwise feeding and growing well?
No fever, vomiting, blood in stool or lethargy?

standard_prescription_format.title: Standard Prescription - Infantile Colic
provisional_diagnosis_options: Excessive crying - rule out organic cause
Possible cow's milk protein sensitivity if indicated
investigation_orders: No routine investigations needed if classic colic and no red flags

medication_template.instructions_en: Use only if symptoms are persistent and after ruling out warning signs
medication_template.instructions_hi: केवल तब दें जब लक्षण बने रहें और खतरे के संकेत न हों

medication_template.instructions_en: May be considered in breastfed infants with persistent colic
medication_template.instructions_hi: स्तनपान करने वाले शिशुओं में लगातार कोलिक होने पर विचार किया जा सकता है

feeding_advice: Do not change formula repeatedly without reason
safety_advice: Seek help if caregiver stress is high
